# FIX v1.2.0

## Prerequisites

- Atlassian CLI (`acli`) installed and authenticated (@.agents/skills/atlassian/SKILL.md)
- Github CLI (`gh`) installed and authenticated (@.agents/skills/gh/SKILL.md)

## Parameters

- **Input (required):** A Jira ticket ID/URL (e.g., `PROJ-123`) **or** a GitHub Issue URL/number (e.g., `https://github.com/Org/repo/issues/42` or `#42`)
- **`--base <branch>`** _(optional):_ Base branch to branch from and target for the PR. Defaults to `main`. Used when implementing stacked PRs for sub-tasks.

## Execution Steps

### 1. Fetch Work Item Details

**Detect input type:**

- If input contains `github.com/` or starts with `#` or is a plain integer → **GitHub Issue mode**
- Otherwise (e.g., `PROJ-123`, `https://invoca.atlassian.net/...`) → **Jira mode**

**GitHub Issue mode:**

```bash
gh issue view <ISSUE-NUMBER-OR-URL> --json number,url,title,body,labels
```

Extract: title, description, acceptance criteria, testing steps, parent issue link (from body), and the Jira ticket ID (from labels or body).

Confirm: _"I found GitHub Issue #<N>: [TITLE]. Proceeding with implementation."_

**Jira mode (original behavior):**

- Use the Atlassian skill (@.agents/skills/atlassian/SKILL.md) to retrieve ticket details
- Extract: title, description, acceptance criteria, and any technical requirements
- Confirm: _"I found ticket [TICKET-NUM]: [TITLE]. Proceeding with implementation."_

### 2. Create Feature Branch

**Branch naming:**

- **Jira mode:** `<ticket-number>/<short-description>` (e.g., `FOO-1234/fix-login-validation`)
- **GitHub Issue mode (standalone):** `gh-<issue-number>/<short-description>` (e.g., `gh-42/add-data-model`)
- **GitHub Issue mode with Jira label** (i.e., called from `/AGENTIC-IMPLEMENT`): use the Jira ID and sub-task index from the issue title, e.g., `<JIRA-ID>/sub-<N>/<short-description>` (e.g., `FOO-123/sub-2/add-api-endpoint`)

Use lowercase with hyphens; keep description concise (3-5 words max).

**Base branch** (use the `--base` parameter if provided, otherwise `main`):

```bash
git fetch origin
git checkout <base-branch>
git pull origin <base-branch>
git checkout -b <branch-name>
```

Confirm branch creation with user.

### 3. Implement Changes

- Analyze the Jira ticket requirements
- Make necessary code changes to address the issue
- Follow existing code patterns and project conventions
- Add/update tests if applicable

### 4. Pre-Commit Validation

- If you are Claude Code and have access to it, run `/simplify`.
- Execute: `.agents/commands/PRE-COMMIT.md`
- **If issues found**:
  - List all issues clearly
  - Fix them automatically where possible
  - Re-run validation until clean
- **If validation passes**: Proceed to step 5

### 5. Commit Changes

- Stage changed files individually (do not use `git add .` — avoid accidentally staging unrelated files or secrets):
  ```bash
  git add <file1> <file2> ...
  ```
- Execute: `.agents/commands/COMMIT.md` to generate and apply commit message
- Verify commit was successful

### 6. Push to Remote

- Push branch to remote:
  ```bash
  git push origin <branch-name>
  ```
- Confirm successful push

### 7. Create Draft Pull Request

- Use the GitHub skill to create a PR with:
  - **Base branch**: The `--base` parameter if provided, otherwise `main`
  - **Head branch**: The newly created branch
  - **Status**: DRAFT
  - **Title**:
    - Jira mode: `[TICKET-NUM] <Jira ticket title>`
    - GitHub Issue mode: `[<JIRA-ID>] <issue title>` (use Jira ID from label if present, otherwise `#<issue-number>`)
  - **Body**: Use `PULL_REQUEST_TEMPLATE.md` from the repository if it exists; populate it with:
    - Link to the GitHub Issue (if in GitHub Issue mode) and/or Jira ticket
    - Description of changes made
    - Manual testing steps (from the issue's Testing section if present)
    - Any relevant notes or rollback considerations
    - **Auto-close keyword (GitHub Issue mode):** Include `Closes #<issue-number>` at the end of the PR body so that GitHub automatically closes the linked issue when this PR is merged into the default branch (`main`). Place it in a dedicated line to ensure GitHub parses it correctly:
      ```
      Closes #<issue-number>
      ```
      > **Important for stacked PRs:** GitHub only triggers issue auto-close when a PR is merged into the repository's **default branch** (`main`). A sub-task PR that currently targets an intermediate branch (e.g., `sub-1`'s branch) will close its issue only after it is ultimately merged into `main` — which happens automatically as each prior PR in the chain is merged in order. Do not remove the `Closes` keyword from stacked PRs; it will take effect at the right time.
- Set appropriate labels if available (e.g., bug, feature, enhancement)

### 8. Final Confirmation

Provide the user with:

```
✅ Draft PR created successfully!

🔗 PR URL: [link]
📋 Jira Ticket: [TICKET-NUM]
🌿 Branch: [branch-name]

⚠️ NEXT STEPS (Human Review Required):
1. Review the code changes in the PR
2. Test the changes locally or in a preview environment
3. Verify all acceptance criteria are met
4. Update the PR description if needed
5. Mark as "Ready for Review" when satisfied
6. Request reviews from appropriate team members

This is a critical human-in-the-loop checkpoint to ensure quality before peer review.
```

## Error Handling

- **Jira ticket not found**: "Unable to find ticket [NUM]. Please verify the ticket number and try again."
- **GitHub Issue not found**: "Unable to find issue [REF]. Verify the issue number and that `gh auth status` is authenticated."
- **Git conflicts**: "There are merge conflicts with `<base-branch>`. Please resolve them before proceeding."
- **Pre-commit failures**: List specific issues and attempt to fix, but escalate to user if unable to resolve automatically
- **Push failures**: Check for branch protection rules or permission issues
- **PR creation failures**: Verify GitHub authentication and repository permissions

## Notes

- Always maintain a conversational tone and keep the user informed at each step
- If any step fails, provide clear error messages and suggested remediation
- Ask for clarification if work item details are ambiguous or incomplete
- Respect existing project conventions (commit message format, code style, etc.)
- When called from `/AGENTIC-IMPLEMENT`, the `--base` branch will be a sub-task branch, not `main` — ensure the branch and PR target are set correctly
